The first is about avoiding peak seasons since they are costly. So option A says "By going there in the peak seasons," which would actually be the opposite of saving money, so A is probably incorrect. Next, the passage says, "Staying outside the resort is a choice that may save you a large amount of money if you plan to stay for a week or two." That directly matches option B. So B is a correct way to save money. Then, about food, the passage says, "Another money saving tip is to buy food ahead of time. If you have most of your meals at your hotel or at cheaper restaurants, it will save you a lot of money. You can try not to buy food in and around Disney World.
